title: Maintenance Log - Pump P-101 Seal Failure
author: Rajesh Kumar
content: Technician Rajesh Kumar performed unscheduled maintenance on Pump P-101. The unit experienced a severe seal leak causing a discharge pressure drop below 4.5 bar. Upon inspection, the primary mechanical seal was found to be completely degraded. This is the third seal leak failure logged for P-101 this year. We replaced the mechanical seal and verified that the discharge pressure recovered to 5.2 bar. Ref: OEM Manual Section 3.2 for seal replacement guidelines.

title: Annual OISD-118 Compliance Audit Report
author: Sanjay Mehta (Safety Lead)
content: Safety audit conducted to verify compliance with OISD-118 regulations at Utility Block B. Pump P-101 was inspected on 2025-06-01 and met all requirements. However, Pump P-102 was found with an outdated inspection record (last inspection: 2024-03-15). Under OISD-118, all pumps handling hydrocarbons must undergo inspections at least every 365 days. Pump P-102 currently has a compliance gap. Also, Compressor C-201, governed by PESO guidelines, is pending inspection. Its last recorded inspection was on 2024-02-10, meaning it also has an active compliance gap.

title: OEM Manual: Centrifugal Pumps P-100 Series
author: Bharat Pumps Ltd
content: Instruction manual for P-100 series centrifugal pumps (models including P-101 and P-102). Section 3.2: Mechanical Seal Maintenance. Preventative maintenance dictates that for high-vibration applications, the mechanical seal must be inspected quarterly. Seal leak events are typically caused by running dry or excessive bearing temperature. If a seal leak occurs, verify the bearing temperature is below 75 C. Section 3.2 recommends annual replacement of mechanical seals to prevent catastrophic failures.

title: Incident Log - Pump P-101 Multiple Seal Failures
author: Amit Sharma
content: Operator Amit Sharma reported an active seal leak on Pump P-101 at 14:30. This was accompanied by a high bearing temperature reading of 82 C, exceeding the 75 C threshold. The pump was immediately tripped. This is another seal leak incident for P-101, which is showing a high failure frequency. On 2025-08-20, during startup, a minor seal leak was again observed. We need a detailed RCA since P-101 has now suffered four seal failures within 12 months.

title: PESO Regulation Compliance Check - Compressor C-201
author: Hitesh Patel
content: Compressor C-201 is governed by PESO regulations due to high-pressure flammable gas handling. A compliance check was executed. The last inspection occurred on 2024-02-10, which exceeds the mandatory 365-day interval required under PESO. A compliance gap is logged. Additionally, the bearing temperature is showing elevated readings of 78 C, which needs to be monitored alongside the vibration level.
